commentary.text: Ross receives outside a pack on the HFF and centres for Hopper to mark in front of Dawson 45m out in the corridor. Hopper shanks left and OOTF.
Peatling's kick clanger in the centre leads to Lefau marking 40m out on the flank from Burton's inside 50 pass, but he misses.
Borlase loses his feet in a contest to allow Lynch to mark a long ball to the square for a goal to stop the rot.
Toby Murray gets a third contact free on Gray 50m out on a slight angle but tries a pass that doesn't work.
Toby Murray gets another free, this one for high contact 35m out on a slight angle, he kicks this one. Junk time is now in session with that goal, six on the trot for the Crom.
Toby Murray misses a set shot from 35m on the flank.
Cook intercepts on the HFF and kicks to the fat side where Isaac Cumming marks over Vlastuin 30m out on a slight angle. The elder Cumming finishes well.
Balta marks 50m out on the flank, his kick draws from right to left but Lefau ends up touching it on the line to deny a goal!
Dawson draws an arm chop free on Miller going for a Neal-Bullen pass to 30m on a slight angle, Dawson's kick is not pretty but effective.
Taylor catches Vlastuin HTB on outer wing, Pedlar kicks to the hotspot to the advantage of Thilthorpe who shoots to the right.

#####The cupboard is bare at Tigerland with 17 names on the injury list, all but two of those with an estimated outage of three weeks or more. Tim Taranto returned, at least, but they decided to manage Samuel Grlj and brought in Luke Trainor.
Jordan Dawson rejoins Adelaide, but they lost Taylor Walker and Jordon Butts with Showdown injuries and brought in second-stringers to replace them.

It was a quiet start for the Crows, who were starting Dawson at half back. By half time, stats were just about even, and a slight margin to the Tigers reflected their shots being taken from better spots. The move of Nick Murray to key forward as cover for Walker was just not working.

After half time, Dawson and Nick Murray reverted to their more comfortable roles, and the Crows woke up with a string of goals to take the lead. Dawson, Izak Rankine, Wayne Milera and Brayden Cook all had prolific quarters to bust the game open.
